instructions: Wash the potatoes, drain them and put them on a tray. Salt them and bake them in an oven, preheated at 170oC, for 45 minutes.
Grate well the cod posts on a stove grill. Remove, let it cool and chip grossly. Reserve.
Cook the grells in salt-tempered water. Drain them well and salt them in a frying pan with a thread of olive oil and a chopped garlic tooth.

lahore-delis-chai.name: Lahore Deli's Chai
instructions: Bring cardamom, fennel seeds, and 1 cup water to a boil in a medium saucepan over medium-high heat. Boil 5 minutes, then reduce heat to medium and whisk in milk and add tea (if using bags, cut open and add tea loose). Simmer, whisking occasionally, until tea is fragrant and a creamy light brown, 8−10 minutes. Strain through a fine-mesh sieve; discard solids. Sweeten tea as desired.

lamb-stir-fry-with-pomegranate-and-yogur.name: Lamb Stir-fry With Pomegranate And Yogurt

ingredients.item: Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper

instructions: Toast cumin and coriander seeds in a small dry skillet over medium heat until fragrant, about 1 minute. Let cool, then finely chop. Toss lamb with cumin, coriander, paprika, garlic, vinegar, and 2 Tbsp. oil in a large bowl to coat; season with salt and pepper. Cover and chill 15 minutes.
Whisk yogurt and 1 Tbsp. water in a small bowl; season with salt and pepper.

Heat remaining 2 Tbsp. oil in a large skillet, preferably cast iron, over medium-high heat. Working in batches, cook lamb, tossing occasionally, until browned, about 5 minutes per batch; transfer to a plate with a slotted spoon.

Add onion to skillet and cook, stirring often, until beginning to brown and soften, about 3 minutes. Add ½ cup water; season with salt and pepper and cook, stirring occasionally, until onion is tender and water is evaporated, about 3 minutes. Return lamb to skillet and toss to combine. Season with salt and pepper.
Serve lamb over rice, topped with yogurt, pomegranate seeds, pistachios, and herbs.
DO AHEAD: Lamb can be marinated 1 day ahead. Keep chilled.

lapardana-teleculinaria.name: Lapardana
instructions: Peel the potato, cut it in slices and bake it in salted water, along with the cabbage. Drain and reserve the cooking water.
Place the cod posts on a tray and put them in the oven, preheated at 180°C, until baked.

Break the bread in pieces and scrape them in the water you've reserved. Bring to the fire a pot with olive oil, chopped garlic teeth, bread run and some pepper and stir. Add the cabbages and potatoes and let cook until you get an açorda.
Have the açorda on a deep platter, place over the roasted cod and water with olive oil. Serve real hot.

last-minute-gravy.name: Last-minute Gravy

ingredients.item: Roasting pan of browned bits and liquid left over from roasting a turkey
ingredients.prep: deglazed with 1 cup water, scraped to loosen (see Maple-Butter-Glazed Turkey)

ingredients.item: Turkey stock or low-sodium chicken broth
. sherry vinegar or red wine vinegar

instructions: Melt 4 Tbsp. unsalted butter in a large saucepan over medium heat. Whisk in 6 Tbsp. (47 g) all-purpose flour and cook, whisking often, until mixture is a shade darker and flour smells nutty, about 5 minutes. Transfer roux to a small heatproof bowl; don’t wipe out pan.
Pour 1 cup dry white wine into same pan and place over medium-high heat. Whisk to incorporate any stray roux left behind and simmer until wine is reduced by three quarters, about 3 minutes.

Add 1 roasting pan of browned bits and liquid left over from roasting a turkey, deglazed with 1 cup water, scraped to loosen; the gravy will be strained later) and 4 cups turkey stock or low-sodium chicken broth. Whisk in roux, taking care to incorporate completely into liquid. Bring to a boil, whisking often, then reduce heat and simmer actively until gravy is thick enough to coat a spoon, 30–45 minutes.

Stir 1 tsp. sherry vinegar or red wine vinegar into gravy if using (if the browned bits in your roasting pan get fairly dark, the vinegar will help brighten and balance those deep savory flavors). Strain gravy through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean saucepan; taste and season with kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper if desired. Keep warm until ready to serve.

learn-how-to-make-fortune-cookies-comede.original_name: Aprende cómo hacer galletas de la fortuna
instructions: Vamos a iniciar nuestra receta mezclando el harina de trigo junto a la maicena y la pizca de sal en un bol . Mezcle muy bien.

leczo.name: Leczo
instructions: In a big pot, fry sausage. Add onion, pepper, and zucchini and fry until vegetables are soft.
Add tomatoes, corn, and spices and cook for 20-30 minutes on low heat.
Serve warm with toasted sourdough bread.

leek-cream-soup-teleculinaria.name: Leek Cream Soup
instructions: Wash and clean the French garlic thoroughly. Cut the white part into small pieces and, in the pan, take them to the stove to saute with 50 g of margarine. Stir with a wooden spoon and before you begin to shine, add the chicken broth.

Peel the potatoes, cut them into little pieces and throw them in the soup. Once cooked, reduce them to puree, preferably with a magic wand. If you don't have it, run it through the Vite Pass. Retrieve the spices.

In a separate bowl, undo the yolk with a little soup and then add it to the pan by stirring well. After taking the yolk, the soup should not boil anymore. Spread the half spoon of margarine, cut into little pieces, over the soup.

From the green leaves of the French garlic cut a small amount into thin strips and, in a separate tachinho, take them to the fire to boil a minute. Drain them and add them to the soup as a garrison. It is very pleasant if, when serving, add small cubes of toasted bread.

leftover-rice-dessert-with-strawberries.name: Leftover Rice Dessert With Strawberries
instructions: Combine strawberries and sugar and set aside to macerate.

Beat cream in a chilled glass or metal bowl using an electric mixer until frothy. Add vanilla sugar gradually, continuing to beat until soft peaks form. Fold in macerated strawberries and rice. Sprinkle with grated chocolate.

lemon-meringue-pie-with-graham-crust.name: Lemon Meringue Pie With Graham Crust
instructions: Preheat oven to 191°C. Butter bottom and sides of 9-inch-diameter glass pie plate. Stir first 4 ingredients in medium bowl to blend. Gradually add butter, stirring until moist clumps form. Press dough evenly onto bottom and up sides of prepared pie plate. Bake crust until golden brown and firm to touch, about 20 minutes. Maintain oven temperature.

Meanwhile, whisk sugar and cornstarch in heavy medium saucepan until no lumps remain. Gradually whisk in lemon juice. Add eggs 1 at a time, whisking to blend well after each addition. Stir in finely grated lemon peel. Whisk mixture constantly over medium heat until filling thickens and boils. Remove from heat; whisk to smooth if necessary. Transfer filling to prepared crust. Smooth top.
Using electric mixer, beat egg whites, cream of tartar, and coarse salt in large bowl until frothy. With mixer running, gradually beat in sugar. Continue beating until stiff and glossy.
Spread meringue over warm filling, sealing meringue to crust at edges. Using spatula, form peaks and swirls in meringue.
Bake pie until meringue is golden brown, about 15 minutes. Cool pie completely on rack.
Cut pie into wedges and serve with Blackberry Sauce.

lemon-rose-bellini.name: Lemon Rosé Bellini
instructions: Combine the sorbet with the vodka in a cocktail shaker with ice. Shake vigorously. Add the champagne and stir. Strain into a chilled champagne flute and garnish with floating raspberries.

lemon-sour-cream-cake.name: Lemon Sour Cream Cake
instructions: Preheat the oven to 163°C (165 degrees C). Grease and flour a fluted tube pan, such as a Bundt (R) pan.
Sift together flour, baking powder, and salt in a bowl.

Combine sugar and butter in a large bowl; beat with an electric mixer until smooth and creamy. Add eggs, 1 at a time, beating well after each addition. Add flour mixture in 3 batches, alternating with sour cream, beating batter briefly after each addition. Stir in lemon zest and pour batter into the prepared pan.

Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted into center comes out clean, 55 to 60 minutes. Cool on a wire rack for 5 minutes. Run a table knife around edges to loosen. Invert carefully onto a serving plate or cooling rack. Let cool completely, about 1 hour.
Stir 3 teaspoons lemon juice, powdered sugar, and butter in a small bowl until a thick glaze forms. Drizzle glaze over cooled cake.

lemony-panzanella.name: Lemony Panzanella

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Preheat an outdoor grill for medium heat and lightly oil the grate.
Drizzle 2 tablespoons olive oil over both sides of the focaccia. Place on a grill pan or directly on the grill grate. Toast until nicely browned, flipping as needed, about 5 minutes.

Meanwhile, combine tomatoes, cucumber, onion, bell pepper, olives, banana peppers, basil, oregano, parsley, salt, and pepper in a nonreactive bowl. Add 1/4 cup olive oil, lemon juice, and Dijon mustard; stir until combined.
Remove focaccia from the grill and rub 1 side of each slice with garlic. Tear slices into bite-sized pieces. Add to the vegetables and stir to coat. Serve at room temperature.

lentil-salad-with-tuna-argiro.name: Lentil Salad With Tuna
instructions: For tuna salad lentils, if you do not find quinoa you can use oatmeal that will soak in water for 10 minutes.
Rinse the lentils and boil them in a little water for 20 minutes in low heat, making sure they do not overheat. Drain and allow to cool.
Then rinse the quinoa, drain it into a colander and add it to the pot with two cups of water.
Cover and as soon as it boils lower the heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes to soften. Drain and allow to cool.
Remove the cucumber seeds, cut into small frames and add to the bowl.
Remove stem, fibers and seeds from the peppers and cut into small frames. Add them to the bowl and add all the other ingredients.
Mix very well and pepper.
Serve the salad cold.

linguine-with-green-olive-sauce-and-zest.name: Linguine With Green Olive Sauce And Zesty Breadcrumbs

ingredients.item: Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.prep: finely grated (about  0.5 cup), plus more for serving

instructions: Heat 1 Tbsp. oil in a medium skillet over medium and cook panko, stirring, until golden, about 5 minutes. Season with salt and pepper; transfer to paper towels to drain and toss with dill and lemon zest.
Cook pasta in a large pot of boiling salted water, stirring occasionally, until al dente. Drain, reserving ½ cup pasta cooking liquid.

Meanwhile, mash anchovies and garlic to a paste on a cutting board with the side of a chef’s knife. Combine with parsley, basil, and half of olives and capers in a large bowl. Chop remaining olives and capers and add to bowl, along with remaining ½ cup oil. Mix well; season sauce with salt and pepper.

Add pasta and ¼ cup reserved pasta cooking liquid to sauce. Toss, adding Parmesan a bit at a time, along with more pasta cooking liquid as needed, until sauce coats pasta. Add lemon juice; season with salt and pepper.
Serve pasta topped with panko and more Parmesan.
Do Ahead: Sauce can be made 1 day ahead. Cover with plastic wrap, pressing directly against surface, and chill.

loaded-grill-cheese-beggin-for-bacon.name: Loaded Grill Cheese: Beggin’ For Bacon
instructions: Spread mayo on one side of each slice of bread.
Add the slice of cheddar cheese to one slice of bread.
Add bacon on top of cheese.
Add tomato on top of bacon.
Add onions on top of tomatoes.
On low heat, add sandwich to a pan and grill mayo side down.
Grill for 3–5 minutes or until cheese melts and bread browns.
Enjoy!

loaded-sweet-potato-skins.name: Loaded Sweet Potato Skins
instructions: Pierce each sweet potato a few times and bake at 400˚F (200˚C) for 50 minutes, or until soft.
Let potatoes cool then cut in half lengthwise.
Scoop out potato flesh leaving a thin layer of sweet potato inside, and add flesh to a medium bowl.
Place skins back on baking sheet, drizzle with olive oil and bake at 400˚F (200˚C) for 10 minutes.
Mash sweet potato flesh with milk, salt, and pepper until smooth and creamy.
Remove sweet potato skins from oven, fill each with an equal amount of potato mixture and top with cheese. Bake at 400˚F (200˚C) for 15 minutes until the cheese is melted.
Remove skins from oven and top with crumbled bacon. Serve with sour cream and chives.
Enjoy!

local-style-fish-soup-teleculinaria.name: Local-style Fish Soup
local-style-fish-soup-teleculinaria.original_name: Sopa de peixe à moda da terra
instructions: Light a pot with the water, season with salt and add the fish. Let it cook and drain, enjoying the broth of cooking. Let the fish cool down, remove skins and spines and deflate it.
Peel the onion and garlic teeth and pick them out. In a pot heat the olive oil, add the onions and garlic and let it saute, without flattering. Add the tomato to pieces and let it reduce a little.
Then add the cooking water to the strained fish and when it starts to boil add the mussel. Apart from that, dissolve the instant clam soup into a little water and add it to the broth.
Finally add the shredded fish, the pyri and the corianders. Let it boil up, retrieve the spices and serve.
Suggests who knows - To further enrich your soup, you can add 100 g of macaroni.

louisana-style-crispy-chicken-recipe-lez.name: Louisana Style Crispy Chicken Recipe

ingredients.item: 500 gr chicken sky or but (in the case of microbial, mice or strip)
Chicken can use chicken but instead; but it is softer and juicy.
If you wish, you can use a little chicken piece.
You can use almond milk, soy milk or lactose-free milk instead of milk.
If you don’t like the egg, you can also cover 1 tablespoon of corn starch + 3 tablespoons of water mixture.
Instead of white flour you can use full wheat flour or gluten-free flour mixture.
You can also get fret using oats.
You can use sweet paprika or f&uuml instead of red powder pepper.
If you like a pepper, you can choose painful variants.
You can use fresh garlic rendesi or onion powder instead of garlic powder.
Depending on the request, you can enrich the flavor with dried herbs such as cake, pepper or fesle.
You can use corn oil, canola oil or olive oil (middle heat) instead of for a monthflower oil.
If you are going to cook in the oven, you can throw the chickens with light-and-eye olive oil.

instructions: 1. Wash and dry chickens.
2. Big polish you want to small parts.
3. Egg in a bowl, milk and bitter sauce fry.
4. Find chickens in this mixture.

9. Paper towels.
10. You can serve with sos or ranch sauce in Louisiana usul&uuml.

louisiana-crawfish-bisque.name: Louisiana Crawfish Bisque

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Melt butter in a large pot over medium heat. Cook and stir onion and green onion in hot butter until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add crawfish tails, condensed soups, canned corns, and cream cheese; cook, stirring often, until cream cheese melts and ingredients are well combined, about 5 minutes.
Stir in half-and-half and Creole seasoning; season with salt and black pepper. Cook until hot, about 15 minutes.

lumaconi-with-prosciutto-and-lemon-bread.name: Lumaconi With Prosciutto And Lemon Breadcrumbs

ingredients.prep: removed in strips with a vegetable peeler

ingredients.item: . lumaconi (snail shells) or other medium shell pasta

instructions: Pulse prosciutto in a food processor until finely ground. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Cook prosciutto, stirring often, until brown and crisp, 5–8 minutes.
Add shallot and garlic to skillet and cook, stirring often, until shallot is soft and garlic is golden brown, about 3 minutes. Add brandy and cook until skillet is almost dry, about 30 seconds.

Add thyme, bay leaf, lemon zest, and cream to skillet and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer until cream is slightly reduced, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat and let steep 30 minutes. Strain through a fine-mesh sieve into a medium bowl; discard solids.
Prosciutto cream can be made 1 day ahead. Cover and chill.

Heat oil in a medium skillet over medium-high heat. Add garlic and cook just until golden, about 2 minutes. Add breadcrumbs and cook, stirring constantly, until golden brown, about 4 minutes. Using a slotted spoon, transfer to paper towels to drain; let cool.
Discard garlic and transfer breadcrumbs to a medium bowl. Add lemon zest and toss to combine; season with salt.
Breadcrumbs (without lemon zest) can be cooked 1 day ahead. Store airtight at room temperature. Add lemon zest just before serving.

Pulse prosciutto in a food processor until finely ground. Heat 1 Tbsp. oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Cook prosciutto, stirring often, until brown and crisp, 5–8 minutes. Add prosciutto cream to skillet and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer, stirring often, until cream is thickened and coats the back of a spoon, about 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook pasta in a large pot of boiling salted water, stirring occasionally, until al dente, 8–10 minutes; drain.
Add pasta to sauce. Cook over medium heat, tossing, until pasta is well coated, about 2 minutes. Add radicchio, parsley, and lemon juice and toss to combine; season with salt.
Serve pasta topped with breadcrumbs and drizzled with more oil.
